Name: Agas & Tamar
Description: Since 1998, a Tel Aviv jewelry institution. Pieces featured in Vogue, Cosmopolitan, and People — with collaborations spanning Sex and the City and a dedicated Donna Karan collection in New York.

Name: AKA My Culture
Description: A curated selection of international eco-conscious labels — DR. DENIM, THINKING MU, BAGGU — alongside the in-house brand IN BLOOM, crafting casual Israeli fashion with an environmental conscience.

Name: Alef Alef
Description: Contemporary Israeli womenswear with a focus on quality, considered design, and the enduring appeal of the everyday.

Name: Alembika
Description: Fluid silhouettes and considered Israeli design for women who move through the world with ease and intention.

Name: Aluma
Description: Former London Fashion Week designer specialising in handmade knitwear. Collections have sold at Selfridges, Harrods, and Neiman Marcus — slow fashion at its most considered.

Name: Anat Friedman
Description: Precise, quietly elegant Israeli womenswear. Anat Friedman's designs speak in restraint — each piece a study in deliberate simplicity.

Name: Anjaly
Description: Effortless unisex loungewear and activewear for women and men — from yoga mat to street, comfort without compromise.

Name: Artwear
Description: Wearable art through bold, original prints. Each Artwear piece turns the body into a canvas — vivid, unapologetic Israeli design.

Name: Assaf Peleg
Description: Designing for the elegant, multi-faceted woman — garments born from emotion and a deep belief in personal style. Assaf Peleg sees the woman before he sees the fabric.

Name: Atawear
Description: A revival of Israel's iconic ATA textile brand, first founded in 1935. The original 'Made in Israel' spirit — workwear heritage reimagined for contemporary street culture.

Name: Aviva Zilberman
Description: A high-end ready-to-wear house founded 2008. Designing for the elegance every woman possesses — a dialogue between masculine precision and feminine sensuality, laid on the body with extreme care.

Name: Belle and Sue
Description: Israel's pioneering online fashion boutique since 2010 — curating international labels, championing local designers, and launching their own FEZ brand of considered everyday essentials.

Name: Biliblond
Description: Israeli swimwear and beachwear with a playful spirit — designed for the Mediterranean lifestyle.

Name: Bracha Baron
Description: Contemporary Israeli womenswear with a refined aesthetic — each collection a careful conversation between structure and ease.

Name: Ciporkin by Dalit
Description: Bauhaus Tel Aviv meets Japanese aesthetics. Geometric, architectural cuts in flowing fabrics — asymmetric, monochromatic, textured. Limited seasonal editions designed by Dalit Ciporkin.

Name: Co.Co
Description: Collective Collection — a collaborative Israeli womenswear label where strong individual voices become a unified design statement.

Name: Comme il Faut
Description: Original, artistic Israeli womenswear — monochromatic palettes, unexpected textures, and asymmetric construction. Comme il Faut dresses the woman who refuses the obvious.

Name: Cotton
Description: Three decades of Israeli womenswear built on natural fabrics, local production, and unwavering quality. Cotton is a synonym for fashion that respects both the woman and the planet.

Name: Dana Ashkenazy
Description: Easy, considered Israeli womenswear for the modern everyday — designed with an eye for proportion and a commitment to quiet confidence.

Name: Dalit Peer
Description: Israeli womenswear by designer Dalit Peer — soft, considered silhouettes with a quiet, contemporary hand.

Name: Dikla Levsky
Description: Printed silk scarves and high-end accessories with a painterly, editorial sensibility. Dikla Levsky makes accessories that define an entire look.

Name: Dorin Frankfurt
Description: One of Israel's most recognised fashion names — Dorin Frankfurt's work blends European sophistication with a distinctly Israeli sensibility.

Name: Dostix
Description: Blending modern urban life with practical, dynamic design. The Dostix brand has established international selling points across Europe, Japan, and the USA.

Name: Flo by Ella Braitman
Description: High-quality women's fashion in limited series, with fine European fabrics. Ella Braitman trained at Moscow's Theatre Design College before studying at Shenkar — and every garment carries that depth of craft.

Name: Forest
Description: Tel Aviv's green lung of fashion — drawing from forests, oceans, and outdoor culture. Original prints and considered silhouettes, produced in small editions by local manufacturers in Tel Aviv.

Name: Frett
Description: A designer knitwear studio: timeless design, fine natural fibres, responsible production in limited quantities. Minimalistic, effortless pieces ethically crafted in Europe.

Name: Gala
Description: Israeli womenswear label with a refined, contemporary point of view — considered silhouettes and quietly confident essentials.

Name: Galler
Description: Women's fashion with bold patterns, optimistic prints, and playful asymmetry. Galler brings colour and joy to the Israeli fashion scene.

Name: Gertrud
Description: Classy, comfortable Israeli womenswear. day to evening, casual and chic

Name: Gon Surfing
Description: Tel Aviv surf culture meets street fashion. Oversized silhouettes for women and men who live between the ocean and the city.

Name: Hagit Tassa
Description: Contemporary Israeli womenswear focused on denim and versatile day-to-night dressing — clean lines, confident construction.

Name: Heroic
Description: Womenswear built around the perfect pair of pants — comfortable, flattering fits that carry you from morning to night without compromise.

Name: Hila Feinberg
Description: Minimalist, oversize Israeli womenswear for those who dress with intention. Hila Feinberg creates space for the woman inside the garment.

Name: Holyland Civilians
Description: Edgy Mediterranean streetwear and activewear with a clubwear edge — designed for all genders, for the track, the rave, and everywhere between. Body-con, all-black, and unapologetically bold.

Name: CALA
Description: Tel Aviv boutique chain founded 2002 — fashion that listens. A personal, intuitive approach to style selection, celebrating individuality at every size, every age, every occasion. Known for its strong vintage and seasonal collections.

Name: JOR-D
Description: Every garment is a canvas, every design a message. Tel Aviv-based statement fashion with a collaborative spirit — from studio appointments on Remez Street to drops with Blundstone and beyond. Wear the story.

Name: Itay Brands
Description: It's all about the basics. A beloved Israeli chain offering sharp, affordable everyday wear for women and men — clean cuts, great fits, and the kind of wardrobe staples you reach for every day. (by Claude)

Name: NewG Lingerie
Description: Founded by Alex, NewG exists to solve the lingerie problem for larger cup sizes who want pretty, comfortable and supportive bras. Wire-free bras in every size and style, from everyday to nursing to sport, designed with real bodies in mind.

Name: Maya Bash
Description: Established fashion designer with over 20 years of experience. Maya Bash combines minimalist, gender-neutral aesthetics with precise tailoring, conceptual innovation, and socially conscious values - multidisciplinary, sustainable, and deeply collaborative.

Name: On'nanoko
Description: Seasonal Israeli fashion from Kibbutz Ma'agan Michael — collections like ECLIPSE and PULSE that push a refined, considered aesthetic for women and a growing menswear line. Quietly one of Israel's most distinctive voices.

Name: OM-ME
Description: Tel Aviv activewear and basics for the in-between moments. OM-ME designs for real bodies - sustainable fabrics, local production, and the kind of comfort you reach for every day. Store on Ben Yehuda 96.

Name: Blueberry TLV
Description: A fresh Tel Aviv label with a playful, considered approach to everyday womenswear — vibrant, wearable, and distinctly local.

Name: Hila Shemesh
Description: Israeli womenswear with a careful, artisan-led approach — each piece considered, each season deliberate. Hila Shemesh designs for the woman who values craft over noise.

Name: IDEE
Description: Israeli design rooted in idea — IDEE creates womenswear that lives in the space between structure and ease, with a clean, modern sensibility.

Name: Ugly Duckling TLV
Description: A Tel Aviv fashion house born in summer 2008 — specialising in flattering women's swimwear with a retro-nostalgic spirit, plus lightweight, comfortable lingerie made and available year-round.

Name: Dodo Bar Or
Description: Tel Aviv's most internationally celebrated bohemian label. Dodo Bar Or's floral, feminine, and free-spirited collections are stocked at Net-a-Porter and Matchesfashion — a beloved name on the global luxury circuit.

Name: Maskit
Description: Founded in 1954 by Ruth Dayan, Maskit is Israel's first and only haute couture house — preserving traditional crafts while dressing the world's most influential women. Revived in 2014 by Sharon Tal (Shenkar / LANVIN / Alexander McQueen), it now spans Haute, RTW, and a curated multi-artist shop.

Name: Pioo Pioo
Description: Founded in 2008 by Mor Valin (Shenkar), Pioo Pioo became an overnight phenomenon in Israeli fashion. Since 2020 every piece is made to order — you become a design partner in your own garment. Exclusive, creative, utterly original.

Name: Lazyeyeppl
Description: A Tel Aviv label with a distinctive editorial eye — considered, original Israeli fashion that refuses easy categorisation.

Name: Kedem Sasson
Description: Dramatic, sculptural Israeli womenswear — Kedem Sasson designs garments that command a room, with architectural precision and an unapologetic sense of theatre.

Name: Kimka Studio
Description: Kim Dror's studio specialises in indigo-dyed clothing and textiles, informed by traditional Japanese mastery and global indigo cultures. Clothing, accessories, and home objects crafted in Haifa with radical care.

Name: Lara Rosnovsky
Description: Kimono-inspired, colourful, and deeply feminine — Lara Rosnovsky's patterned loungewear and beachwear bring a romantic, painterly quality to the Israeli wardrobe.

Name: Layou
Description: Size-inclusive Israeli womenswear built on oversize silhouettes, textured fabrics, and considered minimalism — designed for real wardrobes, real women, and the full spectrum of occasions.

Name: Liah by Liat Raziel
Description: Liat Raziel designs from a belief that fashion is both a necessity and self-expression. Liah pieces are comfortable, tactile, and joyful - women's wear that makes you feel exactly like yourself.

Name: Macchia Studio
Description: Natural tie-dye, organic fabrics, and ethically produced activewear and loungewear - Macchia Studio blends an earthy, free-spirited aesthetic with a conscious approach to production.

Name: Madam Bubu
Description: Relaxed, achromatic, and body-celebrating - Madam Bubu designs clothes that feel like they already know you. Generous sizing (38–46), premium fabrics, and an aesthetic rooted in personal honesty.

Name: Jessa Grey
Description: Size-inclusive Israeli basics — oversized, comfortable, and endlessly versatile. Jessa Grey designs for the wardrobe foundation: the pieces you reach for every day without thinking.

Name: Karen Mizrahi
Description: Israeli womenswear for day and night — elegant, wearable, and considered. Karen Mizrahi designs with confidence and a deep understanding of the modern woman's wardrobe.

Name: Karen Shavit
Description: Fun, colourful, and size-inclusive Israeli womenswear with a joyful, optimistic spirit. Karen Shavit designs for real women who want to be noticed — and to feel good doing it.

Name: Gartenbanken Shoes
Description: Israeli-designed women's shoes and sandals — considered, wearable, and crafted for the streets of Tel Aviv.

Name: Kisim
Description: Bags, wallets, purses, and backpacks with a distinctive Israeli design sensibility — functional objects made beautiful.

Name: The Little Camel
Description: Colourful, artsy wallets, purses, and accessories with a handcrafted Israeli soul — small objects with a big personality.

Name: Justine Hats
Description: Israeli millinery — handcrafted hats for every occasion and every mood.

Name: Take a Nap
Description: Soft, minimalist loungewear and homewear in beautifully textured fabrics designed for the sacred moments of rest.

Name: Shirly Halperin
Description: Founded in 2004 on Zamnehoff Street, Tel Aviv — a small independent label grown into a unique clothing and lifestyle brand. Classical, clean-cut, simple, and comfortable clothes for women, and occasionally men and children.

Name: Maayan Paz
Description: Israeli womenswear since 2012 — minimalist lines, flattering cuts, and luxurious fabrics. Maayan Paz designs the effortlessly polished wardrobe: day-to-evening pieces made in Israel with uncompromising quality.

Name: Kid A
Description: Size-inclusive basics and bold prints — Kid A is for the Israeli woman who believes in comfort as a political act.

Name: Haia
Description: Contemporary Israeli womenswear with a considered, creative approach.

Name: Roni Bar
Description: Wear Yourself - Roni Bar's Tel Aviv studio (84 Ben Zvi Rd) crafts womenswear that moves with its wearer. Clean silhouettes, quality fabrics, and a quiet confidence across tops, pants, dresses, and skirts.

Name: Chopshop Corp
Description: Unorganized. Unruly. Unmissable. Tel Aviv's cult street clothing brand with stores on Dizengoff, Bograshov, Sheinkin and Simtat Hashuk — graphic tees, wide-leg pants, merch, and a fanzine that keeps the culture alive.

Name: Plazmalab
Description: An artist collective making psychedelic and urban tribe clothing - handmade in-house with a true passion for art. Trance-inspired prints, festival-ready hoodies, kimonos, pareos, and more. Abstract designs and wearable art from the lab.

Name: Pattern Streetwear
Description: Streetwear rooted in bold pattern and graphic design - standout pieces for those who wear their personality on their sleeve.

Name: The Freeder
Description: Born from a dancer's borrowed basketball pants — baggy, practical, with big pockets. FREEDÉR blends 90s coolness with 21st-century finesse: sportswear that moves between the studio, the court, and the street. Live the team spirit. Move freely, lightly, boldly.

Name: Razili
Description: Israeli womenswear with a clean, confident identity — considered design rooted in quality craftsmanship and an understated, elegant aesthetic.

Name: TRES TLV
Description: Tel Aviv contemporary label spanning T-shirts, denim, blazers, dresses, and jewellery. Two flagship stores on Ahad Haam and Neot Afeka - TRES has built a loyal community around clean, wearable Israeli design.

Name: Hadas Mor
Description: Breathing Lingerie — a thoughtful Israeli lingerie brand based in Pardes Hanna. Hadas Mor designs bras, bodysuits, swimwear, and underwear with a focus on comfort, femininity, and the kind of beauty that breathes.

Name: SAAG
Description: Contemporary womenswear and accessories - tops, dresses, pants, skirts, and a strong accessories offering including hats, bags, and earrings. Fresh, approachable, and consistently on point.

Name: Tula
Description: Tula by Danielle Maoz, everyday womenswear, relaxed silhouettes, great pants, and a seasonal optimism. Designed and produced in Israel from Emek Hefer.

Name: Inhale Exhale
Description: Israel's first yoga muslin blanket — soft, natural, breathable. Inhale Exhale creates yoga blankets, shawls, pareos, and T-shirts printed with original illustrations by Israeli women artists. Designed and made in Israel with ecological printing technology.

Name: Ripple Yoga Wear
Description: Yoga and movement wear designed for flow - soft, breathable fabrics in calm, considered palettes for studio and everyday.

Name: Miss Nori
Description: Easy, wearable pieces with a soft, feminine sensibility and considered everyday silhouettes.

Name: Vered Grebelsky
Description: Slow-fashion womenswear made in Israel - functional pieces meant to live in a wardrobe for years. Fabric folds, hand embroidery, versatile cuts that wear multiple ways, and a generous size range.

Name: Nataly Shevilly
Description: Designer label by Nataly Shevilly - elegant, occasion-ready pieces with a feminine, considered hand.

Name: Dorit Sade
Description: Israeli womenswear with quiet originality — Dorit Sade designs for women who dress with intention and wear their clothes on their own terms.

Name: Gelada
Description: Cool tshirt prints and more

Name: Con.fail
Description: Israeli label working in a streetwear-meets-conceptual register — playful, slightly off-kilter pieces that break the rules on purpose. (by Claude)

Name: Maya Negri
Description: Israeli womenswear designer creating soft, considered silhouettes with a quiet, contemporary hand.

Name: Mishi
Description: Israeli label Mishi - easy, considered everyday pieces with a clean, modern silhouette. (by Claude)

Name: Nina by Mili
Description: Soft, feminine sensibility and contemporary tailoring.

Name: Keren Wolf
Description: Keren Wolf — Israeli accessories label known for handcrafted bags and shoes with a sculptural, contemporary point of view.

Name: Ustabelle
Description: Founded in 2016 as a children's label, Ustabelle expanded into womenswear in 2023 — premium denim, cashmere blends, and timeless tailored basics, with custom-dyed fabrics that go through two rounds of quality control before reaching your wardrobe.

Name: Gottex
Description: Israel's most iconic swimwear house since 1956 — Gottex dressed Hollywood and royalty long before the country had a fashion industry. Today: full collections from Turkiz to Flirtt, plus modest, plus-size, and cover-up lines.

Name: Neta Efrati
Description: Evening-wear designer with a flagship on Dizengoff Square - Neta Efrati specializes in occasion dresses, jumpsuits, and tailored suits with a precise, considered hand. The Israeli wardrobe for prom, bride and beyond.

Name: Raven
Description: Built to flatter the female body and stay comfortable from gym to street to office. Raven closes the gap between the studio mirror and real life — clothes that feel just as good walking out the door as they did when you tried them on.

Name: Sheron Designs
Description: Sculptural, boho festival design from Sheron - pieces that move between art and apparel.

Name: Ayoola
Description: Israeli womenswear with a soft, contemporary sensibility — Ayoola designs everyday pieces with thoughtful proportion and a quiet confidence.

Name: OZ Capsule
Description: A capsule-wardrobe philosophy - OZ Capsule builds tightly edited collections of considered essentials, made to last across seasons. (by Claude)

Name: Yodfat
Description: At the entrance to Yodfat in the Galilee, set among oak trees with breathtaking views. Founded by Michal Ezroni and Aviv Galon, curates an exclusive edit of designed colorful socks, and selected brands clothing

Name: Toppatsu
Description: Playful, graphic Israeli design with a Japanese-leaning sensibility - Toppatsu makes the kind of pieces you reach for when you want to feel a little more yourself.

Name: Shahar Avnet
Description: Couture-leaning designer Shahar Avnet - known internationally for tulle, hand-painting, and dreamlike eveningwear that blurs costume, art, and fashion. Worn by Beyoncé, Lady Gaga, and Lizzo.

Name: Assemble
Description: Contemporary Israeli streetwear with a tightly considered aesthetic — Assemble designs garments that read clean, modern, and distinctly local.

Name: Barbatana
Description: Israeli streetwear label trading in cheeky local humor — Barbatana's tees lean on Hebrew puns, in-jokes, and a distinctly Israeli sense of mischief.

Name: Bewear
Description: Israeli dancewear brand built around the idea that once you're a dancer, you're a dancer forever. Bewear designs for the studio and beyond — advanced performance fabrics and considered cuts engineered to enhance the dance experience while flattering your presence on stage and off.

Name: Bring Wear
Description: Israeli unisex everyday wear with a relaxed, contemporary sensibility — Bring Wear builds pieces meant to travel from morning to night without changing. (by Claude)

Name: Bazz and Lutra
Description: A whimsical Israeli design duo — Bazz and Lutra make playful, pattern-rich pieces with a strong creative voice and a sense of mischief.

Name: House of Shine
Description: House of Shine — Israeli occasion-wear with shimmer, sequins, and statement silhouettes for the nights you want to be seen.

Name: Sugar & Spice TLV
Description: Tel Aviv-based label with a sweet-meets-sharp edge — Sugar & Spice TLV designs for the woman who plays with contrast.

Name: Studio Oh Design
Description: An Israeli design studio working at the intersection of considered womenswear and craft-led production — clean lines, quality fabrics, and a calm sensibility.

Name: Magia
Description: Tshirts, prints, creative edgy designed tshirts and more

Name: Uniform Apparel
Description: 100% natural fabrics. No synthetics, ever. Founded 2019 between California and Tel Aviv as a reaction to the plastic in fast fashion - a capsule wardrobe of cotton, linen, silk, and wool basics meant to be mixed, matched, and worn for years.

Name: Ulla
Description: Comfortable, practical basics - no chasing trends, no seasons. Clean cuts shaped for the female body across a generous size range (38–48), designed around how the piece will actually wear. The goal: you feel good in it.

Name: Jin-G
Description: Jin-G — Israeli contemporary label working in a relaxed streetwear register with a considered, design-forward eye.

Name: Luka Chari
Description: Israeli men's basics, born from a search for clothes that feel truly right. Founded by a former special-forces officer turned businessman, Luka Chari makes comfortable, precise everyday pieces you don't have to think about, built for morning, evening, and Israeli life in between.

Name: Subtle Body
Description: A practice shaped to evoke unique states of mind, drawing from imagined utopian futures. Each concept is its own self-contained category - a mental, physical, or emotional experience with its own visual line and philosophy.

Name: Saisei
Description: Saisei - meaning rebirth in Japanese - label rooted in regeneration: considered fabrics, mindful production, and clothes designed to be lived in for years.

Name: Zikger
Description: Israeli womenswear label Zikger — considered, contemporary pieces with a calm, design-led sensibility.

Name: Orly Yaron
Description: Designer Orly Yaron's eponymous Israeli label — independent womenswear with a personal, considered point of view.

Name: Yael Orgad
Description: Designer Yael Orgad's eponymous Israeli label — considered womenswear with a careful, contemporary point of view.

Name: Cottna
Description: Israeli period and everyday underwear in soft cotton — Cottna designs reusable, leak-proof basics built for comfort, sustainability, and real life.

Name: Slimz
Description: Shapewear and second-skin essentials - Slimz designs sleek, sculpting basics built to layer invisibly under everything.

Name: Vague Black
Description: Founded 2016 by Anuk Yosebashvili and Yaron Shmerkin. Genderless, seasonless, and almost exclusively black — bold silhouettes and functional details that refuse the cycles of fashion. Defined by the lack of definition.

Name: House of Jaffa
Description: House of Jaffa — considered Israeli fashion with a refined, design-led point of view, rooted in the spirit of its namesake city.

Name: Made by Har
Description: Independent Israeli label by designer Har — small-batch womenswear made with care, with a quiet, contemporary hand.

Name: Petit Pois Studio
Description: A small design studio with a soft, considered aesthetic - Petit Pois makes thoughtful pieces in limited runs.

Name: Doron Ashkenazi
Description: Designer Doron Ashkenazi's eponymous Israeli label — considered, refined womenswear with a sharp eye for proportion and finish.

Name: Tal Maslavi
Description: Designer Tal Maslavi's Israeli jewelry house - fine, sculptural pieces designed to feel at once contemporary and heirloom.

Name: Mishimono
Description: Israeli womenswear with a Japanese-inflected sensibility. Mishimono works in considered silhouettes, soft layering, and a quiet, modern hand.

Name: 7 Generations
Description: Israeli label thinking past the season — designs made to last seven generations, with an emphasis on natural materials, slow craft, and quiet endurance. (by Claude)

Name: Myki Boutique
Description: A small boutique label - understated everyday pieces designed for soft layering and easy living.

Name: Studio Noa
Description: Independent Israeli studio working in clean, considered silhouettes — quietly modern womenswear made in small batches. (by Claude)

Name: Pasiim
Description: Textured fabrics, hand-finishes and a playful but grounded approach to print and pattern.

Name: Ofnat Bracha
Description: Designer Ofnat Bracha young and fun

Name: Jul Fashion
Description: Independent Israeli womenswear — modern, wearable pieces designed for layering and long use.

Name: Zucker Studio
Description: Foundational basics for the modern urban wardrobe — timeless, seasonless, and indifferent to trends. Quality fabrics, precise cuts, an eternal palette. Built to last, priced to wear.

Name: Hosh Studio
Description: Children's clothing studio by Noy Shapira - vintage-inspired, shaped by everyday life as a mother. Bold color, ease, and a non-gendered approach. The collection is designed as a whole so kids can mix freely and make their own choices. Name from hoshi (Japanese: star) and hosh (Arabic slang: something good).

Name: Toda & Co
Description: TODA - Hebrew for 'thank you' - plays the in-between. Punk and graffiti energy on one side, Biblical scenes and scripture on the other. Oversized prints, words you can't ignore, Jewish symbols at scale. Clothes that wear the experience of being different out loud, instead of hiding it.

Name: Frau Blau
Description: Tel Aviv label by designer Helena Blaunstein — maximalist Israeli womenswear with a strong sense of print, color, and storytelling.

Name: Kianne Frankfurt
Description: Designer Kianne Frankfurt — Israeli womenswear with a refined, considered hand and a focus on craft and material.

Name: OT Couture
Description: Sometimes a single letter tells the whole story. OT Couture — Hebrew for 'Letter Couture' — sits at the intersection of fashion, design, and typography. Each piece carries a designed letter that turns it into something personal: which version of yourself are you bringing into the room?

Name: Reggie Studio
Description: (by Claude)

Name: Bow M
Description: Israeli label working in considered, modern silhouettes — quiet pieces with a careful hand. (by Claude)

Name: White Nilus
Description: Born from a deep connection to nature and raw materials - timeless jewelry in silver, gold and natural stones, made to pass from generation to generation. Quality first, so pieces last for years. Jewelry as applied art - an object that encourages people to express their personality.

Name: Kaisra Design
Description: Born from the search for a precise balance - garments that feel like a second skin, wrapping and exact, letting you project presence effortlessly. Designed by listening deeply to the female body and its movement. No passing seasons - timeless pieces inspired by the past and built for whatever's next, with fine materials and uncompromising finish.

Name: Tetra Jewelry
Description: Handmade unisex silver jewelry by Guy Schwartz - original, wild, asymmetrical pieces born from inner inspiration and observation of the environment. Each piece is unique, produced as a limited series, blending roughness and softness, masculine and feminine, into one composition.

Name: Halel
Description: Small Israeli label - thoughtfully made everyday pieces with a quiet, modern hand.

Name: Jollori
Description: Independent Israeli studio - playful, considered pieces with an artistic sensibility. (by Claude)

Name: All At Once
Description: Israeli label - small-batch womenswear with a quiet, considered point of view. (by Claude)

Name: Lika Swimwear
Description: Israeli swimwear designed for real bodies and Mediterranean summers - flattering cuts, modern silhouettes.

Name: Banan Hot
Description: Playful Israeli label - bold prints, bright colors, and pieces with a sense of humor.

Name: 101 Brand Jeans
Description: Israeli denim label - everyday jeans designed for fit, comfort, and a clean, modern silhouette.

Name: 2sis
Description: Israeli womenswear by two sisters - small-batch pieces with a clean, contemporary hand.

Name: Rosery Jewelry
Description: Handcrafted Israeli jewelry - delicate, considered pieces designed for everyday wear. (by Claude)

Name: Omer Ash
Description: Founded in 2022 in Atlit by Omer Ash at 18, a self-taught designer who learned sewing and pattern-making at home. Every collection is handcrafted from quality raw materials, drawing on personal moments and childhood memories. Made in Israel start to finish. Physical store: 'Fabric To Die In', Shenkin 13, Tel Aviv.

Name: Bein Lebein
Description: Bein Lebein by Nadav Dror — a multidisciplinary Israeli label built around hand-screenprinted cotton, heavy-weight natural fabrics (200-400gsm) and small-batch local production. Made to speak in 'our language,' rooting fashion in Israeli culture rather than borrowing from elsewhere.

Name: Andro Lomusia
Description: Established in 2021 and rooted in personal struggle and pain, Androlomusia reflects and reacts to the industry, societal norms, and current world events — building something new with no biases attached. Cut-and-sewn pieces, constructed from the ground up and often purposely damaged by hand.

Name: Jetski Fashion
Description: Born from a deep desire to encourage people to embrace their uniqueness. Innovative, colorful designs that bridge physical and digital, nostalgia and the future, nightlife and everyday wear. Inclusive across body types, genders, and cultures - fashion as a tool for self-expression. (by Claude)

Name: Sdom TLV
Description: Tel Aviv streetwear by artist and designer Stav Greenhouse - illustration, graphic design and contemporary street culture woven into one visual language. Each piece begins with a real story from the city: its energy, contradictions, coastline and nightlife, drawn by hand and built to be lived in.

Name: Nine to Five TLV
Description: Tel Aviv eyewear born from a small dream - making stylish, quality frames accessible at every price point. What started as one shop has grown into a curated home for trend-forward, considered eyewear with no compromise on quality, comfort, or design.

Name: Ori Tamir
Description: Self-taught silversmith Ori Tamir works in 925 silver, without molds - each piece treated as its own object with its own respect. Every piece ships with an accompanying text: a kind of diary of feelings and experiences, with creating as the way to release them.

Name: Maim Jewelry
Description: Israeli jewelry studio - delicate, considered pieces inspired by water and natural form.

Name: Hagomels
Description: Independent Israeli label - small-batch, considered pieces with a quiet, modern hand. (by Claude)

Name: Fly Rooster
Description: Israeli streetwear with attitude - graphic-led pieces and a confident, contemporary point of view.

Name: Hagar Satat
Description: Israeli designer Hagar Satat - considered, sculptural womenswear with a refined, modern hand.

Name: Mystified Labor
Description: Conceptual Israeli label - pieces that turn process into form, working at the edge of fashion and art.

Name: Cor-e
Description: Israeli womenswear - clean, considered silhouettes designed for soft layering and easy wear.

Name: Semek Studio
Description: A place for those who still believe in love, and for those who gave up on it. SEMEK was born from a song the founder wrote after her first heartbreak - the line 'there's no logic in love' became the first collection, then a community. Sculptural, emotional, handmade.

Name: Eladan
Description: Israeli label - thoughtful everyday pieces made with care and a contemporary hand. (by Claude)

Name: Shuzi Jewelry
Description: Designed and handcrafted in Israel by Sharon Gendelman - fresh, unique pieces inspired by Mediterranean culture and the sea. Sterling silver, gold, pearls and seashells, always chic and easy to layer.

Name: Haptic Path
Description: Israeli studio working at the intersection of craft, material, and form - tactile, considered objects. (by Claude)

Name: Yoske
Description: Designed by Dorin Cohen Nissan in memory of her grandfather Yoske. A connection to nature, family and roots - a brand built around stories, moving experiences, and meetings of hearts. Not streetwear, not ready-to-wear: 'life wear,' a brand of life.
